item.title: Italian court rules Netflix price-hike clauses are void, orders refunds
item.description: A Rome court has ruled as unlawful price increases imposed by Netflix ‌on its Italian subscribers over the last seven years, and has ordered the U.S. media giant to reimburse them, a consumer association said on Friday.

item.title: China's Space Pioneer says reusable rocket's maiden flight fails
item.description: Beijing-based rocket developer Space Pioneer ​said on Friday that the maiden flight of its reusable rocket Tianlong-3 failed, highlighting the ‌challenge China's rocket developers face in catching up to Elon Musk's SpaceX.

item.title: Senators urge Trump to bar Chinese automakers from building cars in US
item.description: Three Democratic senators on Friday urged President Donald Trump to bar Chinese automakers from building vehicles in ‌the United States and to prevent Chinese cars assembled in Mexico or Canada from entering the United States.

item.title: Turkey launches investigation into Google over advertising, billing practices
item.description: The Turkish Competition ​Board said on ‌Friday it had decided to launch ​an investigation ​into Alphabet Inc and ⁠related Google ​companies over its ​advertising and billing practices.

item.title: United Airlines to introduce tiered fare categories for premium cabins
item.description: United Airlines said on Friday that it plans to introduce a new tiered fare structure in premium cabins starting ​this year.

item.title: Deepseek’s V4 model will run on Huawei chips, The Information reports
item.description: China's DeepSeek's new model called V4 ​will run on the ‌latest chips designed by Huawei Technologies, U.S. digital ​news outlet The ​Information reported on Friday.

item.title: Trump administration considering expanding Chinese tech gear crackdown
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission said Friday it ​is considering banning the ‌import of Chinese equipment from a group of manufacturers ​after previously barring approvals ​of new models in 2022.

item.title: Car site Edmunds tests a Chinese SUV, says it should worry US automakers
item.description: Popular car-shopping website Edmunds put a Chinese vehicle through a series of rigorous tests ​for the first time, and Editor-in-Chief Alistair Weaver came away with a notable impression: U.S. automakers have good reason for concern.

item.title: VIEW US jobs report for March is stronger than expected, likely keeping Fed on sidelines
item.description: U.S. nonfarm payrolls expanded more than Wall Street anticipated last month and the jobless rate fell to 4.3%, bolstering expectations that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady as it gauges economic growth, inflation and the impact of the war with Iran.

item.title: US employment growth rebounded in March; unemployment rate falls to 4.3%
item.description: U.S. job growth rebounded more than expected in March as a strike by healthcare workers ended and temperatures warmed ​up, and the unemployment rate fell to 4.3%, but downside risks to the labor market are mounting from a ‌war with Iran that has no clear end in sight.

item.title: Consumer megadeals make a rare comeback in the first quarter
item.description: Two blockbuster mergers involving U.S. food companies, struck within 24 hours, gatecrashed ​the ranks of the biggest global transactions of the first quarter, a feat not seen in the consumer sector in over a decade.

item.title: US targets Chinese chipmaking with proposed export restrictions on ASML and others
item.description: A cross-party group of U.S. politicians have proposed ‌a law to impose further restrictions on exports of computer chipmaking equipment to China, affecting companies such as ASML and China's top chipmakers.

item.title: China regulator orders food delivery giants to strengthen safety controls
item.description: China's market regulator met with major ​food delivery platforms ‌this week, ordering them to strengthen food safety measures ​ahead of new ​regulations taking effect in ⁠June.

item.title: Moscow shoppers and travellers hit by payment system problem
item.description: A problem with payment systems sowed chaos on Friday for some ​Russian shoppers, forcing the turnstiles on ‌the Moscow metro to allow people to enter without payment and even forcing a regional ​zoo to ask visitors to ​use cash.

item.title: Cambodian parliament passes landmark cybercrime law after scam centre scrutiny
item.description: Cambodia's parliament on Friday passed the first law dedicated to targeting scam centres accused of bilking international victims out of billions of dollars, as pressure mounts on governments ​to tackle the illicit businesses.

item.title: China moves to regulate digital humans, bans addictive services for children
item.description: China's cyberspace regulator issued draft regulations on Friday to oversee the development online of digital humans, requiring clear labelling and banning services ​that could mislead children or fuel addiction.

item.title: Wall St Week Ahead Inflation in focus for markets jostled by Middle East war signals
item.description: A fresh read on inflation and initial company results next week could start to show the Middle East war's effects ​on the U.S. economy and corporate America, as investors hope to start moving past a conflict that has consumed markets.

item.title: North American farmers pinch pennies on farm machinery as profitless growing season approaches
item.description: Farm machinery salespeople are wrapping up ​a dismal season of farm shows across North America as farmers gear up for spring planting without much new equipment.

item.title: Samsung Elec likely to report stupendous surge in quarterly profit to record level
item.description: Samsung Electronics , benefiting from red-hot chip prices stoked by the AI boom, is expected to book a six-fold jump in operating ​profit for January-March - a quarterly record and just shy of what it earned for all of the past business year.

item.title: Hyundai Motor flags export disruptions as Middle East conflict hits shipping
item.description: Hyundai Motor said on Friday that exports to Europe and North Africa, which typically transit through ‌the Middle East, were being disrupted by the conflict in the region, underscoring growing strains on global supply chains.

item.title: Kremlin's drive for a state-backed messenger touches a nerve for some
item.description: The Kremlin is pushing Russians to use state-backed messenger MAX, but some are wary about the service and say they do not want to download it at all.

item.title: Tesla eyes Japan's top imported-car spot as it expands store, service network
item.description: Tesla plans to expand in ​Japan to at least 60 stores as it seeks to become the country's biggest imported ‌car brand by as early as next year, the head of its Japan business said on Friday.

item.title: US employment growth likely rebounded in March, war casting shadow over labor market
item.description: U.S. job growth likely rebounded in March as a strike by healthcare workers ended and temperatures warmed up, but downside risks to the labor market are mounting from the dragging war ​in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ to raise rates with eye on Iran war fallout, central bank official says
item.description: The Bank of Japan will continue to raise interest rates while keeping a close ​eye on how the Middle East conflict affects ‌the economy and underlying inflation, a senior central bank official said on Friday.

item.title: Microsoft to invest $10 billion in Japan for AI and cyber defence expansion
item.description: Microsoft on Friday said it will invest 1.6 trillion yen ($10 billion) in ​Japan between 2026 and 2029 to expand ‌artificial intelligence infrastructure and strengthen cybersecurity cooperation with the government.

item.title: China services activity growth cools in March, private PMI shows
item.description: Growth in China's services activity slowed in March from February's 33-month high, as softer demand and a decline in overseas orders weighed on momentum, a private-sector survey showed on Friday.

item.title: Under global spotlight, Australia plays hardball on social media ban
item.description: Since Australia banned children from using social media in December, lawmakers from Spain to Malaysia have expressed interest in following suit, while U.S. courts have found ​tech firms negligent toward young users.

item.title: A year after 'Liberation Day,' Trump sets new drug tariffs, adjusts metals duties
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered 100% tariffs on certain branded pharmaceutical imports and overhauled steel, aluminum and copper duties on Thursday as his ​administration sought to move on from the collapse of the broad global tariffs he announced exactly one year ago.

item.title: Exclusive: Oil giants show early interest in US Gulf deepwater field stake, sources say
item.description: European energy majors TotalEnergies and Shell are among companies eyeing a majority stake in one of the U.S. Gulf's most promising sites, three ​sources with knowledge of the process said, as interest in North American energy prospects ‌rises due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Starbucks closes China deal with Boyu, plans to expand stores
item.description: Starbucks has closed its deal with Boyu Capital to ​sell control of its China ‌operations, the Seattle-based coffee company announced Thursday.
